behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both the file and the environment, the error message names the missing key by its exact config path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is file-config + env-override + validation/error-format handoff, not just one pure function (MOCK-MISS). No real OS/network/subprocess required, so not System (INTEGRATION-BRAKE a). No AI output dependency, so not Agentic/Workflow (b,c). Rejected higher level because behavior text is only about missing-key reporting, not real infra or model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real config-loader modules with in-process fixtures: provide config missing `database.host`, empty relevant `TOOL_` env, run startup load path, assert failure and exact path `database.host` in message.

behaviors.description: When the environment variable `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, the loader resolves the config file path relative to that directory rather than `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: This is path-resolution logic driven by env input at a single boundary; real failure can be caught by testing resolver function/object directly (MOCK-MISS says function logic stays Unit). No real subprocess/network needed; no model dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Call path-resolution function with `XDG_CONFIG_HOME=/tmp/custom` (and controlled home), assert computed path is `/tmp/custom/tool/config.yaml` and not `~/.config/tool/config.yaml`.

behaviors.description: When an environment variable with the `TOOL_` prefix conflicts with a key in the config file, the environment variable value is used.
behaviors.justification: Precedence bug appears at module handoff between file parse and env overlay (MOCK-MISS), so Unit on one function is insufficient unless merger is fully isolated. No required real OS/network/subprocess (INTEGRATION-BRAKE a=no). No AI dependency or multi-agent chain (b,c,d=no). Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is deterministic precedence logic only.
behaviors.test_description: Run loader stack with a config fixture containing key `database.host=file-value` and env `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST=env-value`; assert resulting config uses `env-value`.

behaviors.description: When the config file contains a YAML syntax error, the error message includes the line number where the problem was detected.
behaviors.justification: Needs real interaction between YAML parser exception and loader error formatter; mocking parser can hide real exception-shape failures (MOCK-HIDE). Still local/in-process, so System is not strictly required unless testing OS-level file failure modes. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Feed malformed YAML through the real parser path used by loader (fixture file or in-memory reader), trigger startup load, assert emitted error contains parse line number from parser output.

behaviors.description: When the config directory does not exist at startup, the loader creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on real filesystem side effects (mkdir + file write ordering), an OS primitive (REAL-INFRA). In-process fakes may miss permission/path/creation semantics (MOCK-HIDE). Not Agentic/Workflow (no model decisions). Rejected Integration because behavior text requires actual directory/file creation at startup.
behaviors.test_description: In an isolated temp HOME/XDG path with no config dir, run CLI startup path end-to-end, assert directory now exists, defaults file exists with expected baseline content, and startup continues past load stage.
